In [5]:
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score

# ── Load normalization ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
s_min = np.load('s_min.npy')
s_max = np.load('s_max.npy')

# ── Rebuild models ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
device = torch.device('cpu')

fwd_net = ForwardNet()
fwd_net.load_state_dict(torch.load('best_forward_net_v2.pth', map_location='cpu'))
fwd_net.eval()

inv_net = InverseNet()
inv_net.load_state_dict(torch.load('best_inverse_net_v2.pth', map_location='cpu'))
inv_net.eval()

print("Models loaded")

# ── Load a sample of test data ────────────────────────────────────────
spectra    = np.load('spectra.npy').astype(np.float32)
structures = np.load('structures.npy').astype(np.float32)

n = len(spectra)
idx      = torch.randperm(n, generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(42))
n_train  = int(0.70*n)
n_val    = int(0.15*n)
idx_test = idx[n_train+n_val:].numpy()

# Use 500 test samples for plots
test_spec   = spectra[idx_test[:500]]
test_struct = structures[idx_test[:500]]

struct_norm = (2*(test_struct - s_min)/(s_max - s_min) - 1).astype(np.float32)

with torch.no_grad():
    fwd_pred  = fwd_net(torch.tensor(struct_norm)).numpy()          # forward predictions
    inv_pred_norm = inv_net(torch.tensor(test_spec)).numpy()        # inverse predictions

inv_pred_nm = (inv_pred_norm + 1) / 2 * (s_max - s_min) + s_min
true_nm     = test_struct

wl = np.linspace(0.40, 0.705, 61)
labels = ['T_amor', 'R_amor', 'T_crys', 'R_crys']
colors = ['#2196F3', '#F44336', '#4CAF50', '#FF9800']

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PLOT 1: Forward Network — 6 random test spectra
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
fig, axes = plt.subplots(6, 4, figsize=(18, 18))
for row in range(6):
    for col in range(4):
        ax = axes[row][col]
        ax.plot(wl*1000, test_spec[row][col::4],
                color=colors[col], lw=2, label='S4 True')
        ax.plot(wl*1000, fwd_pred[row][col::4],
                color='black', lw=1.5, ls='--', label='NN Pred')
        ax.set_title(f'Sample {row+1} | {labels[col]}', fontsize=8)
        ax.set_xlabel('Wavelength (nm)', fontsize=7)
        ax.set_ylabel('Intensity', fontsize=7)
        ax.legend(fontsize=6)
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Forward Network: True S4 Spectra vs Neural Network Prediction\n(6 test samples)',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plot1_forward_spectra.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
plt.close()
print("Plot 1 saved")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PLOT 2: Forward Network — MSE distribution across 500 test samples
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
mse_per_sample = np.mean((fwd_pred - test_spec)**2, axis=1)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

axes[0].hist(mse_per_sample, bins=50, color='#2196F3', edgecolor='white', alpha=0.8)
axes[0].axvline(mse_per_sample.mean(), color='red', lw=2,
                label=f'Mean MSE = {mse_per_sample.mean():.6f}')
axes[0].set_xlabel('MSE per sample', fontsize=11)
axes[0].set_ylabel('Count', fontsize=11)
axes[0].set_title('Forward Network: MSE Distribution (500 test samples)', fontsize=11)
axes[0].legend(); axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

axes[1].hist(mse_per_sample, bins=50, color='#2196F3', edgecolor='white', alpha=0.8)
axes[1].axvline(mse_per_sample.mean(), color='red', lw=2,
                label=f'Mean = {mse_per_sample.mean():.6f}')
axes[1].set_xlabel('MSE per sample', fontsize=11)
axes[1].set_ylabel('Count', fontsize=11)
axes[1].set_title('Forward Network: MSE Distribution (log scale)', fontsize=11)
axes[1].set_yscale('log')
axes[1].legend(); axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plot2_forward_mse_dist.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
plt.close()
print("Plot 2 saved")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PLOT 3: Inverse Network — R² scatter per layer
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
r2 = r2_score(struct_norm, inv_pred_norm, multioutput='raw_values')

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(18, 9))
layer_materials = ['SiO2','Sb2S3','SiO2','Sb2S3','SiO2','Sb2S3','SiO2','Sb2S3']

for i, ax in enumerate(axes.flat):
    true_vals = true_nm[:, i]
    pred_vals = inv_pred_nm[:, i]
    ax.scatter(true_vals, pred_vals, alpha=0.15, s=6,
               color='#3F51B5', rasterized=True)
    ax.plot([25, 200], [25, 200], 'r--', lw=2, label='Perfect')
    ax.set_xlim(20, 205); ax.set_ylim(20, 205)
    ax.set_xlabel('True (nm)', fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel('Predicted (nm)', fontsize=9)
    ax.set_title(f'Layer {i+1} ({layer_materials[i]})  R²={r2[i]:.3f}',
                 fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')
    ax.legend(fontsize=7); ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    ax.set_aspect('equal')

plt.suptitle(f'Inverse Network: True vs Predicted Layer Thicknesses\nMean R² = {r2.mean():.3f}',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plot3_inverse_scatter.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
plt.close()
print("Plot 3 saved")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PLOT 4: R² bar chart per layer
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
bar_colors = ['#4CAF50' if v > 0.85 else '#FF9800' if v > 0.65 else '#F44336'
              for v in r2]
bars = ax.bar([f'L{i+1}\n{layer_materials[i]}' for i in range(8)],
              r2, color=bar_colors, edgecolor='white', linewidth=1.2)
ax.axhline(r2.mean(), color='navy', lw=2, ls='--',
           label=f'Mean R² = {r2.mean():.3f}')
ax.axhline(0.85, color='green', lw=1, ls=':', alpha=0.7, label='R²=0.85 target')

for bar, val in zip(bars, r2):
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2, bar.get_height() + 0.01,
            f'{val:.3f}', ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')

ax.set_ylim(0, 1.1)
ax.set_ylabel('R² Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Inverse Network R² per Layer\n(Green > 0.85 | Orange 0.65–0.85 | Red < 0.65)',
             fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10); ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='y')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plot4_r2_bars.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
plt.close()
print("Plot 4 saved")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PLOT 5: S4 Verification results — load from file
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
try:
    import pandas as pd
    df = pd.read_csv('verification_results.txt', sep='\t')
    print(df.head())

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 5))

    axes[0].bar(df['Sample'], df['MSE'], color='#2196F3', edgecolor='white')
    axes[0].axhline(df['MSE'].mean(), color='red', lw=2, ls='--',
                    label=f'Mean = {df["MSE"].mean():.4f}')
    axes[0].set_xlabel('Sample'); axes[0].set_ylabel('MSE')
    axes[0].set_title('S4 Verified MSE per Sample')
    axes[0].legend(); axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    axes[1].bar(df['Sample'], df['MAE'], color='#4CAF50', edgecolor='white')
    axes[1].axhline(df['MAE'].mean(), color='red', lw=2, ls='--',
                    label=f'Mean = {df["MAE"].mean():.4f}')
    axes[1].set_xlabel('Sample'); axes[1].set_ylabel('MAE')
    axes[1].set_title('S4 Verified MAE per Sample')
    axes[1].legend(); axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    axes[2].bar(df['Sample'], df['MaxErr'], color='#FF9800', edgecolor='white')
    axes[2].axhline(df['MaxErr'].mean(), color='red', lw=2, ls='--',
                    label=f'Mean = {df["MaxErr"].mean():.4f}')
    axes[2].set_xlabel('Sample'); axes[2].set_ylabel('Max Error')
    axes[2].set_title('S4 Verified Max Error per Sample')
    axes[2].legend(); axes[2].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    plt.suptitle('S4 Verification Results — 20 Test Samples', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig('plot5_s4_verification.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
    plt.show()
    plt.close()
    print("Plot 5 saved")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Plot 5 skipped (verification_results.txt issue): {e}")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PLOT 6: One detailed spectrum comparison — True vs Predicted vs S4
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
sample_idx = 0
true_s   = test_spec[sample_idx]
fwd_s    = fwd_pred[sample_idx]

fig = plt.figure(figsize=(16, 10))
gs  = gridspec.GridSpec(2, 2, hspace=0.4, wspace=0.3)

for col, (lbl, c) in enumerate(zip(labels, colors)):
    ax = fig.add_subplot(gs[col//2, col%2])
    ax.plot(wl*1000, true_s[col::4],  color=c,     lw=2.5, label='S4 Ground Truth')
    ax.plot(wl*1000, fwd_s[col::4],   color='black', lw=1.5,
            ls='--', label='Forward Net Prediction')
    ax.fill_between(wl*1000,
                    true_s[col::4], fwd_s[col::4],
                    alpha=0.15, color=c, label='Error region')
    ax.set_xlabel('Wavelength (nm)', fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel('Intensity', fontsize=10)
    ax.set_title(lbl, fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
    ax.legend(fontsize=8); ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Detailed Spectrum Comparison: S4 Ground Truth vs Forward Network\n'
             f'(Test Sample 1 | MSE = {np.mean((true_s - fwd_s)**2):.6f})',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
plt.savefig('plot6_detailed_spectrum.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
plt.close()
print("Plot 6 saved")

print("\n✅ All plots saved:")
for i in range(1, 7):
    print(f"  plot{i}_*.png")
